Ye shall weep and lament (\klausete kai thrˆnˆsete\). Future active of \klai"\ and \thrˆne"\, both old words (for \klai"\ see John 11:31, for \thrˆne"\ see Matthew 11:17), both words used of the loud lamentations so common in the east. Shall rejoice (\charˆsetai\). Picture the women on the way to the Cross (Luke 23:27) and Mary Magdalene by the tomb (John 20:11, \klaiousa\). Ye shall be sorrowful…

He sought no reward for his work in this world except the place of glory he had with the Father "before the world was." "I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father." (John 16:28.) Could anything be written with greater plainness? Then followed the Savior's solemn discourse when He said to the Eleven: "These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world." (John 16:33.)

[The Savior said]: “These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.” (John 16:33) The peace that He spoke of is defined by one writer: “True joy is an intense inner peace and happiness.” It is the peace that Paul spoke of, “the peace of God, which passeth all understanding.” The gospel of…

Be of Good Cheer and Faithful in Adversity By Elder Adhemar Damiani Of the Seventy The gospel of Jesus Christ gives us the strength and the eternal perspective to face what is coming with good cheer. “Peace is the message that came when the Savior was born a Babe in Bethlehem.
